title: clusia minor
intro: Clusia minor, also known as the "bloodwood" or "clusia", is a plant species that belongs to the family Clusiaceae, a group of flowering plants native to Central and South America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to stop bleeding and reduce inflammation, due to the presence of anthraquinones and other bioactive compounds. Clusia minor has been used to treat various health issues, including wounds, diarrhea, and fever, as well as to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. In herbal preparations, the plant can be made into a decoction or infusion, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
